tools.name: iamPolicies.iamIamPoliciesLintPolicy
tools.description: Lints, or validates, an IAM policy. Currently checks the google.iam.v1.Binding.condition field, which contains a condition expression for a role binding. Successful calls to this method always return an HTTP `200 OK` status code, even if the linter detects an issue in the IAM policy.

tools.name: iamPolicies.iamIamPoliciesQueryAuditableServices
tools.description: Returns a list of services that allow you to opt into audit logs that are not generated by default. To learn more about audit logs, see the [Logging documentation](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/audit).

tools.name: locations.iamLocationsWorkforcePoolsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new WorkforcePool. You cannot reuse the name of a deleted pool until 30 days after deletion.

tools.name: locations.iamLocationsWorkforcePoolsList
tools.description: Lists all non-deleted WorkforcePools under the specified parent. If `show_deleted` is set to `true`, then deleted pools are also listed.

tools.name: permissions.iamPermissionsQueryTestablePermissions
tools.description: Lists every permission that you can test on a resource. A permission is testable if you can check whether a principal has that permission on the resource.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new WorkloadIdentityPool. You cannot reuse the name of a deleted pool until 30 days after deletion.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsList
tools.description: Lists all non-deleted WorkloadIdentityPools in a project. If `show_deleted` is set to `true`, then deleted pools are also listed.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsProvidersCreate
tools.description: Creates a new WorkloadIdentityPoolProvider in a WorkloadIdentityPool. You cannot reuse the name of a deleted provider until 30 days after deletion.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsProvidersKeysCreate
tools.description: Create a new WorkloadIdentityPoolProviderKey in a WorkloadIdentityPoolProvider.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsProvidersKeysList
tools.description: Lists all non-deleted WorkloadIdentityPoolProviderKeys in a project. If show_deleted is set to `true`, then deleted pools are also listed.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsLocationsWorkloadIdentityPoolsProvidersList
tools.description: Lists all non-deleted WorkloadIdentityPoolProviders in a WorkloadIdentityPool. If `show_deleted` is set to `true`, then deleted providers are also listed.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsRolesCreate
tools.description: Creates a new custom Role.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsRolesList
tools.description: Lists every predefined Role that IAM supports, or every custom role that is defined for an organization or project.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsCreate
tools.description: Creates a ServiceAccount.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsGetIamPolicy
tools.description: Gets the IAM policy that is attached to a ServiceAccount. This IAM policy specifies which principals have access to the service account. This method does not tell you whether the service account has been granted any roles on other resources. To check whether a service account has role grants on a resource, use the `getIamPolicy` method for that resource. For example, to view the role grants for a project, call the Resource Manager API's [`projects.getIamPolicy`](https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v1/projects/getIamPolicy) method.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysCreate
tools.description: Creates a ServiceAccountKey.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysDelete
tools.description: Deletes a ServiceAccountKey. Deleting a service account key does not revoke short-lived credentials that have been issued based on the service account key.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysDisable
tools.description: Disable a ServiceAccountKey. A disabled service account key can be re-enabled with EnableServiceAccountKey.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysEnable
tools.description: Enable a ServiceAccountKey.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysList
tools.description: Lists every ServiceAccountKey for a service account.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsKeysUpload
tools.description: Uploads the public key portion of a key pair that you manage, and associates the public key with a ServiceAccount. After you upload the public key, you can use the private key from the key pair as a service account key.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsList
tools.description: Lists every ServiceAccount that belongs to a specific project.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsPatch
tools.description: Patches a ServiceAccount.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsSetIamPolicy
tools.description: Sets the IAM policy that is attached to a ServiceAccount. Use this method to grant or revoke access to the service account. For example, you could grant a principal the ability to impersonate the service account. This method does not enable the service account to access other resources. To grant roles to a service account on a resource, follow these steps: 1. Call the resource's `getIamPolicy` method to get its current IAM policy. 2. Edit the policy so that it binds the service account to an IAM role for the resource. 3. Call the resource's `setIamPolicy` method to update its IAM policy. For detailed instructions, see [Manage access to project, folders, and organizations](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/service-accounts/granting-access-to-service-accounts) or [Manage access to other resources](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/access/manage-other-resources).

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsSignBlob
tools.description: **Note:** This method is deprecated. Use the [`signBlob`](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/rest-credentials/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/signBlob) method in the IAM Service Account Credentials API instead. If you currently use this method, see the [migration guide](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/credentials/migrate-api) for instructions. Signs a blob using the system-managed private key for a ServiceAccount.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsSignJwt
tools.description: **Note:** This method is deprecated. Use the [`signJwt`](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/rest-credentials/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/signJwt) method in the IAM Service Account Credentials API instead. If you currently use this method, see the [migration guide](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/credentials/migrate-api) for instructions. Signs a JSON Web Token (JWT) using the system-managed private key for a ServiceAccount.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsTestIamPermissions
tools.description: Tests whether the caller has the specified permissions on a ServiceAccount.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsUndelete
tools.description: Restores a deleted ServiceAccount. **Important:** It is not always possible to restore a deleted service account. Use this method only as a last resort. After you delete a service account, IAM permanently removes the service account 30 days later. There is no way to restore a deleted service account that has been permanently removed.

tools.name: projects.iamProjectsServiceAccountsUpdate
tools.description: **Note:** We are in the process of deprecating this method. Use PatchServiceAccount instead. Updates a ServiceAccount. You can update only the `display_name` field.

tools.name: roles.iamRolesGet
tools.description: Gets the definition of a Role.

tools.name: roles.iamRolesList
tools.description: Lists every predefined Role that IAM supports, or every custom role that is defined for an organization or project.

tools.name: roles.iamRolesQueryGrantableRoles
tools.description: Lists roles that can be granted on a Google Cloud resource. A role is grantable if the IAM policy for the resource can contain bindings to the role.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
